nodes.name: Grilled Chicken Souvlaki Plate
nodes.description: name: Grilled Chicken Souvlaki Plate | short_description: Marinated grilled chicken skewers served with fluffy couscous, a crisp cucumber-tomato salad, and creamy tzatziki — a classic Greek lunch plat
nodes.premise: Meal: Grilled Chicken Souvlaki Plate - name: Grilled Chicken Souvlaki Plate | short_description: Marinated grilled chicken skewers served with fluffy couscous, a crisp cucumber-tomato salad, and creamy tzatziki — a classic Greek lunch plate. | meal_suggestion: Marinate chicken cubes in olive oil, lemon juice, oregano, and garlic for at least 30 minutes. Thread onto skewers and grill until charred. Serve alongside fluffy couscous, a salad of diced cucumber, tomato, red onion, and kalamata olives, and a generous dollop of tzatziki. Add a lemon wedge and warm pita on the side. | nutrition_note: Lean chicken breast is high in protein and low in saturated fat; couscous provides quick-digesting carbohydrates; cucumber and tomato add hydration, vitamins C and K; olives supply monounsaturated fats; tzatziki (yogurt-based) contributes calcium and probiotics. A balanced Mediterranean plate with protein, carbs, healthy fats, and vegetables.

nodes.name: Spanakopita-Style Stuffed Pita
nodes.description: name: Spanakopita-Style Stuffed Pita | short_description: Warm pita pockets filled with a savory spinach-feta mixture inspired by Greek spanakopita — handheld, flavorful, and satisfying. | meal_sugges
nodes.premise: Meal: Spanakopita-Style Stuffed Pita - name: Spanakopita-Style Stuffed Pita | short_description: Warm pita pockets filled with a savory spinach-feta mixture inspired by Greek spanakopita — handheld, flavorful, and satisfying. | meal_suggestion: Sauté fresh spinach with diced onion and garlic in olive oil until wilted. Mix with crumbled feta, a squeeze of lemon, dill, and a pinch of nutmeg. Stuff into warmed whole-wheat pita halves. Serve with a side of Greek salad or hummus and veggie sticks for a complete lunch. | nutrition_note: Spinach is rich in iron, folate, and vitamins A and K; feta adds protein and calcium; whole-wheat pita provides fiber and complex carbohydrates. Olive oil contributes monounsaturated fats for heart health. A lighter take on traditional spanakopita without the phyllo pastry, reducing saturated fat while keeping all the classic flavors.

nodes.name: Herb-Crusted Baked Sea Bass with Roasted Vegetables
nodes.description: name: Herb-Crusted Baked Sea Bass with Roasted Vegetables | short_description: Flaky sea bass baked with a Provençal herb crust, served alongside roasted zucchini, eggplant, and cherry tomatoes drizzl
nodes.premise: Meal: Herb-Crusted Baked Sea Bass with Roasted Vegetables - name: Herb-Crusted Baked Sea Bass with Roasted Vegetables | short_description: Flaky sea bass baked with a Provençal herb crust, served alongside roasted zucchini, eggplant, and cherry tomatoes drizzled with olive oil. | meal_suggestion: Season sea bass fillets with a mixture of breadcrumbs, dried herbs de Provence, lemon zest, and a drizzle of olive oil. Bake at 400°F for 15–18 minutes. Toss zucchini, eggplant, and cherry tomatoes with olive oil, salt, and pepper; roast on a separate tray. Plate the fish over the vegetables and finish with a squeeze of lemon and fresh basil leaves. | nutrition_note: Sea bass is a lean white fish high in protein and rich in selenium and B vitamins; roasted Mediterranean vegetables provide fiber, vitamins A and C, and potassium. Olive oil adds anti-inflammatory monounsaturated fats. Herbs de Provence (thyme, rosemary, oregano) contribute antioxidants. A light, elegant dinner aligned with the Mediterranean diet.

nodes.name: Lamb Kofta with Tabbouleh & Hummus
nodes.description: name: Lamb Kofta with Tabbouleh & Hummus | short_description: Spiced lamb kofta patties served alongside a fresh bulgur tabbouleh salad and creamy homemade hummus — a hearty Mediterranean dinner sprea
nodes.premise: Meal: Lamb Kofta with Tabbouleh & Hummus - name: Lamb Kofta with Tabbouleh & Hummus | short_description: Spiced lamb kofta patties served alongside a fresh bulgur tabbouleh salad and creamy homemade hummus — a hearty Mediterranean dinner spread. | meal_suggestion: Mix ground lamb with minced onion, garlic, cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and fresh parsley; form into small oval patties and grill or pan-sear until cooked through. Prepare tabbouleh by tossing fine bulgur with chopped parsley, mint, tomato, and cucumber in a lemon-olive oil dressing. Serve with a generous scoop of hummus and warm flatbread. | nutrition_note: Lamb provides high-quality protein, iron, zinc, and B12; bulgur wheat is a whole grain rich in fiber and manganese; parsley and mint add vitamins A, C, and K plus fresh flavor. Hummus (chickpeas + tahini) supplies plant protein, fiber, and healthy fats. A satisfying, protein-rich dinner; opt for lean ground lamb to reduce saturated fat.

nodes.name: One-Pot Greek Chicken & Orzo
nodes.description: name: One-Pot Greek Chicken & Orzo | short_description: Tender chicken thighs braised with orzo, tomatoes, olives, and artichoke hearts in a lemony broth — a comforting one-pot Mediterranean dinner. |
nodes.premise: Meal: One-Pot Greek Chicken & Orzo - name: One-Pot Greek Chicken & Orzo | short_description: Tender chicken thighs braised with orzo, tomatoes, olives, and artichoke hearts in a lemony broth — a comforting one-pot Mediterranean dinner. | meal_suggestion: Brown seasoned chicken thighs in olive oil in a deep skillet. Remove and sauté onion and garlic, then add diced tomatoes, chicken broth, orzo, sliced kalamata olives, and canned artichoke hearts. Nestle the chicken back in, cover, and simmer 20 minutes until orzo is tender and liquid is absorbed. Finish with lemon juice, crumbled feta, and chopped fresh oregano. | nutrition_note: Chicken thighs provide protein with more flavor than breast; orzo supplies carbohydrates; tomatoes and artichoke hearts are rich in fiber, vitamin C, and antioxidants; olives contribute monounsaturated fats. A balanced one-pot meal with protein, carbs, and vegetables. Use low-sodium broth to manage salt; skin-on thighs add richness but also saturated fat — remove skin if preferred.

nodes.name: Bean Burrito Bowl — Smoky Twist

For lower fat, use reduced-fat cheddar or reduce cheese; to increase fullness without many calories, add extra salsa or lettuce where possible. | nutrition_note: High in fiber and plant protein from black beans; includes calcium from cheddar and energy from rice. Corn and lettuce add micronutrients; watch sodium and saturated fat from cheese and salsa; portion control helps with calories.

nodes.name: Chicken Noodle Soup — Garden Veg Bowl

To increase fiber and fullness, swap regular noodles for whole-grain noodles and add a handful of leafy greens or chopped spinach. nodes.premise: Meal: Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Smoky Bake - name: Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Smoky Bake | short_description: A one-pan sheet-pan dinner featuring smoky-tossed pork tenderloin, potatoes, and green beans roasted with olive oil for a balanced, convenient meal. | meal_suggestion: Serving idea: 4 oz pork tenderloin, 1 cup potatoes, and 1 cup green beans, roasted with olive oil and smoky spices. nodes.premise: Meal: Turkey Sandwich — Mild-Spice Combo - name: Turkey Sandwich — Mild-Spice Combo | short_description: A mild-spice turkey sandwich on whole-grain bread with lettuce, tomato, and mustard. | meal_suggestion: Keep as a simple, balanced lunch: turkey sandwich on whole-grain bread with lettuce and tomato. Pair with fruit or carrot sticks for more fiber. To adjust spice, use a milder mustard or add a small amount of hot sauce if acceptable. | nutrition_note: Lean turkey provides protein; whole-grain bread adds fiber; lettuce and tomato supply vitamins. Moderate sodium from deli turkey and mustard; consider low-sodium options if needed. Contains gluten (bread).

nodes.name: Egg Fried Rice — Lemon-Herb Skillet
nodes.description: name: Egg Fried Rice — Lemon-Herb Skillet | short_description: Egg fried rice with peas and carrots, seasoned with soy sauce and sesame oil; a quick protein- and veggie-packed skillet meal (lemon-herb
nodes.premise: Meal: Egg Fried Rice — Lemon-Herb Skillet - name: Egg Fried Rice — Lemon-Herb Skillet | short_description: Egg fried rice with peas and carrots, seasoned with soy sauce and sesame oil; a quick protein- and veggie-packed skillet meal (lemon-herb flavor notes optional). | meal_suggestion: One-pan dish ready in minutes. Serve as is or with a side salad or steamed greens. To adjust nutrition, swap in brown rice for more fiber and/or reduce oil; use low-sodium soy sauce to cut sodium. Optional lemon zest and herbs can brighten the flavor. | nutrition_note: Protein-rich egg with rice provides a balanced mix of carbs and protein; vegetables add fiber and micronutrients (vitamin A from carrots, vitamin C from peas). Sesame oil contributes flavor and healthy fats; soy sauce increases sodium, so consider low-sodium soy sauce or reduce amount. For more fiber, use brown rice or add extra veggies; for brighter flavor, add lemon zest/juice and fresh herbs (optional).

nodes.name: Roast Chicken Dinner — Maple-Kissed Bowl

nodes.premise: Meal: Roast Chicken Dinner — Maple-Kissed Bowl - name: Roast Chicken Dinner — Maple-Kissed Bowl | short_description: Roast chicken thighs with quinoa, broccoli, and carrots for a protein-forward, fiber-rich veggie bowl. | meal_suggestion: Serve warm: roasted chicken thighs with fluffy quinoa and steamed broccoli and carrots; the name suggests a maple-kissed flavor—top with a light maple glaze if you like. | nutrition_note: Protein-rich chicken thighs provide satiety; quinoa offers complete protein and fiber; broccoli and carrots add vitamins A and C plus fiber, making this a balanced bowl with moderate fat from the thighs.

nodes.name: Vegetable Soup & Bread — Garden Veg Bowl

nodes.premise: Meal: Vegetable Soup & Bread — Garden Veg Bowl - name: Vegetable Soup & Bread — Garden Veg Bowl | short_description: A fiber-forward garden veggie meal featuring vegetable soup, whole-grain bread, and a fresh side salad. | meal_suggestion: Great as a light, balanced lunch or dinner. For extra protein, add beans, lentils, or hummus to the soup or salad; drizzle a little olive oil on the salad for healthy fats; choose low-sodium soup to manage salt intake. | nutrition_note: Fiber-rich, plant-based meal with vegetables, whole grains, and a side of greens. Provides complex carbs from whole-grain bread and essential vitamins; protein is modest—consider adding a protein source if you need more satiety. Watch sodium levels in soup; opt for low-sodium options when possible.

nodes.name: Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Sesame Skillet
nodes.description: name: Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Sesame Skillet | short_description: One-pan pork tenderloin with potatoes and green beans roasted in olive oil. | meal_suggestion: Serve as a complete dinner: pair with a
nodes.premise: Meal: Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Sesame Skillet - name: Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Sesame Skillet | short_description: One-pan pork tenderloin with potatoes and green beans roasted in olive oil. | meal_suggestion: Serve as a complete dinner: pair with a side salad or steamed greens and a grain (e.g., quinoa) for extra fiber. Season with herbs and a light seasoning of salt if desired. | nutrition_note: Lean pork tenderloin provides high-quality protein; potatoes supply complex carbohydrates; green beans add fiber and micronutrients; olive oil contributes heart-healthy fats. This one-pan meal can fit into a balanced diet when portions are appropriate.

nodes.name: Veggie Quesadilla — Garlic-Ginger Variation

nodes.premise: Meal: Veggie Quesadilla — Garlic-Ginger Variation - name: Veggie Quesadilla — Garlic-Ginger Variation | short_description: A vegetarian quesadilla on a whole-wheat tortilla with cheddar, bell pepper, spinach, and salsa. Fiber and calcium-rich with veggie nutrients, ready in a quick, balanced meal. | meal_suggestion: Pair with a light side salad or fruit to round out the meal; for higher protein, add a lean protein (beans, tofu, or chicken) or include more beans inside the quesadilla; using reduced-fat cheese can lower overall saturated fat. | nutrition_note: Fiber-rich from whole-wheat tortilla and vegetables; provides calcium and protein from cheddar; includes vitamins A, C, and K from peppers and spinach; salsa adds vitamin C. Watch sodium from cheese and salsa; consider reduced-fat cheese or low-sodium salsa to lower saturated fat and sodium.

nodes.name: Caprese Sandwich — Mild-Spice Bowl

To boost protein, add grilled chicken or chickpeas. Pair with fruit or veggie side to round out the meal and keep portions mindful of sodium. | nutrition_note: Fiber from whole-grain bread plus protein and calcium from mozzarella; tomatoes and basil add vitamins. Balsamic adds flavor with modest sugar; watch sodium from cheese. Mild, vegetarian option.

nodes.name: Egg Fried Rice — Mild-Spice Bake
nodes.description: name: Egg Fried Rice — Mild-Spice Bake | short_description: A baked, mildly spiced version of egg fried rice with rice, egg, peas, carrots, soy sauce and sesame oil. | meal_suggestion: Serve warm with
nodes.premise: Meal: Egg Fried Rice — Mild-Spice Bake - name: Egg Fried Rice — Mild-Spice Bake | short_description: A baked, mildly spiced version of egg fried rice with rice, egg, peas, carrots, soy sauce and sesame oil. | meal_suggestion: Serve warm with a side of leafy greens or cucumber salad to balance sodium; for fiber boost, substitute brown rice and opt for low-sodium soy sauce. | nutrition_note: Protein from egg and carbs from rice; includes fiber and micronutrients from peas and carrots. Sesame oil adds healthy fats. Soy sauce adds sodium—use low-sodium soy sauce or reduce amount. For more fiber, swap white rice with brown rice.

Use a low-sodium soup option and drizzle a small amount of olive oil on the salad for healthy fats. Consider adding a piece of fruit for dessert. | nutrition_note: Fiber-rich meal from vegetables and whole grains; provides complex carbs, vitamins, and minerals with low saturated fat. nodes.name: DIY Taco Night — Garlic-Ginger Skillet
nodes.description: name: DIY Taco Night — Garlic-Ginger Skillet | short_description: Lean beef tacos on corn tortillas with fresh veggies and cheddar—balanced, customizable DIY taco night. | meal_suggestion: Assemble 2
nodes.premise: Meal: DIY Taco Night — Garlic-Ginger Skillet - name: DIY Taco Night — Garlic-Ginger Skillet | short_description: Lean beef tacos on corn tortillas with fresh veggies and cheddar—balanced, customizable DIY taco night. | meal_suggestion: Assemble 2 warm corn tortillas and fill with 3–4 oz lean ground beef, shredded lettuce, diced tomato, a tablespoon of salsa, and a light sprinkle of cheddar. Optional: top with avocado for healthy fats. | nutrition_note: Protein-rich lean beef with fiber from lettuce, tomato, salsa and corn tortillas; calcium from cheddar. Monitor sodium from salsa and cheese. For a lighter option, use 2 tortillas, 3–4 oz beef, and low-sodium salsa or reduced-fat cheddar.

Optional lime, avocado, or light sour cream. Pair with a simple side salad or beans for extra fiber. | nutrition_note: Lean beef provides high‑quality protein; corn tortillas supply carbohydrates; lettuce and tomato add volume and micronutrients; cheddar contributes calcium and fat; salsa offers flavor with relatively low calories. To boost fiber, use whole‑grain tortillas or add beans; to reduce sodium, opt for low‑sodium salsa and lighter cheese.
